name: Las Lagunas de Ruidera — circular desde Viveros
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Viveros traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including areas with natural lagoons and historical tunnels. The region offers a mix of moderate and challenging paths, suitable for cyclists seeking both scenic rides and more demanding ascents. Routes often feature rolling hills and open countryside, providing diverse cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Viveros The most popular touring cycling route is Las Lagunas de Ruidera — circular desde Viveros, a 49.6 miles (79.8 km) trail that takes 6 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural lagoons of Ruidera. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Robledo, a moderate 15.9 miles (25.7 km) path. This route features tunnels and forms a loop from Robledo. Local touring cyclists also love the Alcaraz – Tunnels loop from Robledo, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail leading through areas with tunnels, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Touring cycling around Viveros is defined by natural lagoons, historical tunnels, and rolling countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Viveros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Viveros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Viveros?
A: There are over 7 touring cycling routes around Viveros, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Viveros?
A: The touring cycling routes in Viveros are primarily moderate to difficult. You'll find 6 moderate routes and 1 difficult route, suitable for cyclists looking for a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Viveros?
A: The routes around Viveros feature varied terrain, including areas with natural lagoons and historical tunnels. Expect rolling hills and open countryside, providing diverse cycling experiences. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357677534/las-lagunas-de-ruidera-circular-desde-viveros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Ruidera — circular desde Viveros&lt;/a&gt;, explore significant natural features.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Viveros?
A: Yes, several routes in Viveros are circular. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz – Tunnels loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; are popular loops that incorporate historical tunnels.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can explore interesting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo Station&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2971463" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaduct on the Via Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4849870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through other historical tunnels.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Viveros?
A: The touring cycling routes in Viveros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural lagoons and historical tunnels.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that feature tunnels?
A: Yes, several routes prominently feature tunnels. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz – Tunnels loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel – Rest area loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; for routes that include these historical features.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available in Viveros?
A: The longest touring cycling route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357677534/las-lagunas-de-ruidera-circular-desde-viveros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Ruidera — circular desde Viveros&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 80 km (49.6 miles) and typically takes over 6 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any shorter, moderate touring cycling options?
A: For a shorter, moderate ride, you could try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38273676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Café-Bar El Horcajo&lt;/a&gt;. It's about 22.4 km (13.9 miles) long and can be completed in under 3 hours.

Q: What are the elevation gains like on Viveros touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Viveros feature varying elevation gains. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357677534/las-lagunas-de-ruidera-circular-desde-viveros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lagunas de Ruidera — circular desde Viveros&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 470 meters, while moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; involve around 260 meters of ascent.

Q: Is there a touring cycling route that starts from Robledo?
A: Yes, several routes offer starting points or loops from Robledo. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel – Rest area loop from Robledo&lt;/a&gt; for options originating in that area.
tips:
The path that the Ruta del Quijote follows is mostly along forest tracks in good condition, although some sections may be damaged by the rains.
The route is indicated with signs of the Quixote Route and Natural Paths.
The White Lagoon is one of the 15 lagoons that make up the Ruidera lagoons and is known for its crystal clear water and white sand bottom.
A small but beautiful waterfall in the Arroyo de Sabinar, right between the Baño de las Mulas and the Plaza de Toros. It deserves a stop.
These are the salt mines that are more than 600 years old. In the environment you can see flora and fauna that adapted to the salinity.
